---
title: "Results"
---

# Results

Results from the simulation sweep are summarized below.

```{python}
#| label: fig-revenue
#| fig-cap: "Annual revenue under three elasticity scenarios."
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from phillyparking.scenarios.runner import run_all_scenarios
from phillyparking.scenarios.compare import comparison_table
results = run_all_scenarios()
table = comparison_table(results)
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(6, 4))
if 'revenue' in table.columns:
    table['revenue'].plot.bar(ax=ax)
    ax.set_ylabel('annual revenue ($)')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

## Chatman–Manville comparison

Min-vacancy-at-peak targeting reduces cruising DWL relative to average-occupancy targeting; full results in @fig-revenue and the analysis notebook `08_chatman_manville_test.ipynb`.

## Revenue and PBD allocation

Projected annual revenue and the 30% PBD share by zone.
